prompt: A vast dark wooden banquet table viewed from slightly above. The table fills the entire frame. On the left 80% of the table — representing half of all humanity — there is almost nothing at all: completely bare dark wood, one small broken bread crust in the center-left, two or three scattered crumbs, deep cold blue shadow, silence and emptiness. Absolutely no glasses, plates, or food anywhere on this side. On the far right 20% edge only — representing the richest 10% of people — an impossibly excessive feast is crammed and overflowing: gold-rimmed plates stacked high, grapes and figs tumbling off the edge, a tall glowing candle casting warm amber light, a full wine glass. The emotional weight is in the VASTNESS of the empty side. The feast is a tiny sliver of excess. No people, no text, no labels, no axes. Single frame. Oil painting style, cinematic dramatic lighting, magazine cover composition.
insight: The poorest half of humanity shares just 2% of global wealth, while the richest 10% hold 76% — a disparity so extreme that half the table sits completely bare.
